content_markdown: A small spherical planet has radius $R = 2000\text{ m}$ and has no atmosphere. The acceleration due to gravity on the surface of this planet is $2.0\text{ m s}^{-2}$. At an altitude of $3000\text{ m}$ above the surface of the planet the magnitude of the gravitational field strength is

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: Gravitational field strength $g$ follows an inverse-square law $g = \frac{GM}{r^2}$ outside a spherical mass. As the distance from the center of the planet increases from $R$ (at the surface) to $R + h$ (at an altitude), the denominator $r^2$ increases, causing the field strength to decrease. Since $3000\text{ m}$ is further away than the surface, the field strength must be less than the surface value of $2.0\text{ N kg}^{-1}$ but still greater than zero.
